reforms.description: Colombia offers a special R&D tax incentive that allows companies to deduct up to 175% of their investments in scientific and technological projects from their taxable income.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentive encourages private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax incentive is currently in effect and being utilized by companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances the incentive structure for private R&D investment but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Colombia offers a special regime for R&D that allows a deduction of up to 175% for investments in scientific and technological projects.

reforms.description: Colombia has reformed its royalty sharing system to distribute oil and mining revenues more equitably across producing and non-producing regions, aiming to close infrastructure gaps and reduce corruption.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance regional equity and infrastructure, which can foster a more conducive environment for R&D and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been enacted and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how resources are allocated, impacting regional development and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The royalty sharing system (SGR) was reformed in 2012 to spread oil and mining revenues more broadly across producing and non-producing regions.
